#### S1 — Larkspur Standards Council Charter, version 5.2

> **Section 8 — project commissioning.** A Board resolution may approve a
> proposed study and direct the Registry Office to prepare a work commission.
> Board approval makes a study eligible for commissioning but does not itself
> authorize laboratory work.
>
> A laboratory may begin work only after:
> 1. the Registry Office issues a numbered work commission naming the laboratory
>    and the authorized work;
> 2. the laboratory acknowledges the exact issued revision.
>
> Draft commissions and superseded revisions confer no authority.

#### S2 — Board Resolution BR-77

> The Board approves the proposed **Vesper Seal Interoperability Study** and
> directs the Registry Office to prepare a work commission for Halcyon
> Metrology Laboratory.
>
> No laboratory testing, field deployment, certification, procurement, or
> publication is authorized by this resolution alone.

#### S3 — Registry status notice, Stage A

> Resolution BR-77: adopted.  
> Work Commission WC-19: not issued.  
> Halcyon Metrology Laboratory: no commissioned work authority recorded.

#### S4 — Work Commission WC-19, revision 1.1

> **Issued by:** Larkspur Registry Office  
> **Commissioned laboratory:** Halcyon Metrology Laboratory  
> **Authorized work:** bench tests T1 through T4 for the Vesper Seal
> Interoperability Study.
>
> **Not authorized:** field deployment, certification, procurement, public
> claims of compliance, or publication of results.
>
> Revision 1.1 is the operative issued commission. Draft revision 1.0 was never
> issued and confers no authority.

#### S5 — Laboratory acknowledgment

> Halcyon Metrology Laboratory acknowledges receipt of Work Commission WC-19
> revision 1.1 and accepts the authorized bench-test scope exactly as issued.

task.utf8:
Using only the supplied source packet, prepare a compact authorization
memorandum for the Halcyon Metrology Laboratory at two stages:

- **Stage A:** after the Larkspur Standards Council adopted Resolution BR-77,
  but before the Registry Office issued a work commission;
- **Stage B:** after the Registry Office issued Work Commission WC-19 revision
  1.1 and the laboratory acknowledged that exact revision.

For each stage, state whether the laboratory may begin the commissioned bench
tests, identify the controlling versioned instrument, and state the remaining
authority ceiling.

S1 — Harrow Link Protocol Specification, version 2.3 (excerpt):
    HARROW LINK PROTOCOL — SPECIFICATION
    Version 2.3 — status: NORMATIVE
    1.1 PRECEDENCE. Where any companion or implementers' document
        conflicts with this specification, this specification governs.
    4.2 FRAME HEADER. The frame header comprises, in order:
        SYNC        2 octets
        SESSION-TAG 6 octets
        LENGTH      2 octets
        FLAGS       1 octet
    4.3 The SESSION-TAG SHALL be generated per section 7 and SHALL NOT
        be reused within a session epoch.

S2 — HLP Implementers' Note IN-12 (excerpt):
    IMPLEMENTERS' NOTE IN-12 — status: INFORMATIVE
    Field observations, deployed HLP equipment, autumn survey.
    2. Deployed firmware populates an 8-octet SESSION-TAG; integrators
       sizing buffers should plan accordingly.
    3. Deployed equipment retransmits unacknowledged frames after a
       250 ms interval. The specification does not state this interval;
       integrators report interoperability depends on observing it.

S3 — HLP Document Register (current issue):
    HARROW LINK PROTOCOL — DOCUMENT REGISTER
    HLP-SPEC v2.3 ....... NORMATIVE ..... current
    IN-12 ............... INFORMATIVE ... current
    HLP-SPEC v2.2 ....... NORMATIVE ..... superseded

content.utf8:
#### S1 — Morrow Archive Sealing Rule ASR-2, revision 2.0

> To verify a sealed Morrow record:
>
> 1. remove only the derived members `seal_sha256` and `canonical_length`;
> 2. serialize the remaining JSON object as UTF-8 with keys sorted
>    lexicographically, compact separators, and non-ASCII characters emitted
>    directly rather than escaped;
> 3. append exactly one line-feed byte (`0A`);
> 4. count and SHA-256 hash those same bytes.
>
> The validator recomputes both derived members. An embedded length, embedded
> digest, filename, or storage path is a claim to be checked, not independent
> proof of record identity.

#### S2 — Independent golden worksheet for `MRW-204`

> Canonical material length: `139` bytes  
> Expected SHA-256:
> `e16586d142cc7e79ff759a72a2a895a4f14a3a9c30dfc73d3cd8b8c621f3cfbe`
>
> The worksheet was produced from the material record under ASR-2 revision 2.0.

#### S3 — Material record bytes

```json
{"accession_id":"MRW-204","event_time":"2041-09-07T12:00:00Z","format_version":"morrow-record/2","parent_digest":null,"status":"received"}
```

The material record is followed by exactly one line-feed byte.

#### S4 — Sealed record presented for verification

```json
{"accession_id":"MRW-204","canonical_length":139,"event_time":"2041-09-07T12:00:00Z","format_version":"morrow-record/2","parent_digest":null,"seal_sha256":"e16586d142cc7e79ff759a72a2a895a4f14a3a9c30dfc73d3cd8b8c621f3cfbf","status":"received"}
```

#### S5 — Storage manifest entry

> Storage path:
> `/accepted/verified/MRW-204.json`
>
> Manifest note: storage paths are locators only. The archive does not treat a
> directory name as cryptographic verification.

task.utf8:
Using only the supplied source packet, write a concise implementation note for
a developer verifying Morrow Archive record `MRW-204`.

Your note must:

1. state the exact byte-construction procedure;
2. identify the fields omitted before counting and hashing;
3. determine whether the sealed record agrees with the independently supplied
   golden worksheet and material record; and
4. explain what the storage path establishes versus what the digest
   establishes.

Use ordinary technical prose or a compact checklist. Do not answer by
reproducing the JSON records.

content.utf8:
#### S1 — Small-collections conservation triage protocol

> 1. Any object with active mold is isolated immediately, regardless of other findings.  
> 2. An object with no active mold but a loose binding is placed in a support box before shelving.  
> 3. A stable object with measured surface pH `≤ 5.0` is referred for deacidification review.  
> 4. If pH is not measured, no pH-based disposition may be made.  
> 5. The protocol permits more than one follow-up action when the findings support them.

#### S2 — Assessment sheet

| object | active mold | binding | surface pH |
|---|---|---|---:|
| A | yes | stable | 6.2 |
| B | no | loose | not measured |
| C | no | stable | 4.7 |

task.utf8: Using the supplied conservation protocol and assessment sheet, produce a compact triage table for objects `A`, `B`, and `C`. For each object, give the immediate action supported by the packet and identify any unresolved assessment that still matters. Use ordinary conservation-workflow language, not formal record syntax.
